readings.title: TOP500 List (November 2025)
readings.description: The official TOP500 ranking of the world's fastest supercomputers. Updated twice yearly. El Capitan, Frontier, Aurora, and JUPITER all crossed the exascale threshold.

readings.title: El Capitan System Overview
readings.description: LLNL's official page on El Capitan, including architecture details of the AMD MI300A APU and HPE Cray EX255a system design.

readings.title: Frontier User Guide (OLCF)
readings.description: Detailed hardware documentation for Frontier, covering node architecture, MI250X GPU specs, Slingshot interconnect, and Orion file system.

readings.title: Kim, Dally, and Abts: Technology-Driven, Highly-Scalable Dragonfly Topology (ISCA 2008)
readings.description: The original dragonfly topology paper. Introduces the hierarchical topology with global and local links, analyzes minimal and non-minimal routing, and demonstrates cost advantages over fat trees at scale.

readings.title: TPU v4 Architecture (arXiv 2304.01433)
readings.description: Google's paper on TPU v4 architecture, covering systolic arrays, 3D torus interconnect with optical circuit switching, and pod-level performance.

readings.title: Rabenseifner: Optimization of Collective Reduction Operations (ICCS 2004)
readings.description: The foundational paper on the Rabenseifner algorithm for MPI allreduce, combining recursive halving-doubling to achieve both logarithmic latency and near-optimal bandwidth.

readings.title: Thakur et al.: Optimization of MPI Collective Communication (IJHPCA)
readings.description: Comprehensive analysis of MPI collective algorithms including broadcast, reduce, allreduce, and alltoall. Covers the algorithm selection framework used in MPICH.

readings.title: Cerebras CS-3 and WSE-3
readings.description: Cerebras wafer-scale engine specifications: 900,000 AI cores, 44 GB SRAM on-die, 21 PB/s memory bandwidth. Architectural overview of the SRAM-only design philosophy.

readings.title: Al-Fares et al.: A Scalable, Commodity Data Center Network Architecture (SIGCOMM 2008)
readings.description: The fat tree (Clos network) paper that demonstrated how commodity switches can build full-bisection-bandwidth data center networks. Foundation for modern data center networking.
